Q13 NAT Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the polynomial How many symmetric matrices with integer entries are there whose characteristic polynomial is p?

NAT
Correct answer: 0
Solution

0
A real symmetric matrix has real eigenvalues that are all real.
The roots of the square matrix’s characteristic polynomial are the eigenvalues.
The polynomial’s roots are 1, I and –i (non-real).
As a result, NO symmetric matrix with integer entries and the provided characteristic polynomial
exists.

Q15 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A depth-first forest classifies the edges of a graph into tree, back, forward, and cross edges. A breadth-first tree can also be used to classify the edges reachable from the source of the search into the same four categories. In a breadth-first search of an directed graph, which of the following
properties hold?

Correct answer: B, C, D
Solution

(b, c, d)
To have a forward edge, we would need to have already processed a vertex using more than one
edge, even though there is a path to it using a single edge. Since breadth first search always
considers shorter paths first, this is not possible.

Q21 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following relation schema:

 
 
What does the following SQL query return?
Select distinct S.RollNo From Student S Where NOT EXISTS ((Select P.Pcode From Packages P)
Minus (Select R.Pcode From Registers R Where R.RollNo = S.RollNo))
Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
(Select P.Pcode From Packages P) → This selects Pcode of all the packages.
(Select R.Pcode From Registers R Where R.RollNo = S.RollNo) → This selects Pcode of packages that belong to that particular student.
Subtracting second from first will give, Pcode of packages that do not belong to that student. If
this does not exist, then that student will be selected. We can say that a student is selected if there
does not exist any package which does not belong to that student. Or, every package belongs to
that student. Or, student who registered for all the packages.

Q23 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statement(s). Then, which of the following is/are true?

Correct answer: A, B, D
Solution

(a, b, d)
• Maskable Interrupt: An Interrupt that can be disabled or ignored by the instructions of CPU
are called as Maskable Interrupt.
• Non-Maskable Interrupt: An interrupt that cannot be disabled or ignored by the instructions
of CPU are called as Non-Maskable Interrupt.
• Vectored Interrupt: Device causing interrupt provides the starting address of Interrupt Service
Routine (ISR).
• Non-vectored Interrupt: Device causing interrupt does not provides the starting address of
Interrupt Service Routine (ISR).

Q28 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statement is/are correct with respect to stop and wait ARQ?

Correct answer: A, C
Solution

(a, c)
It uses a link between sender and receiver as a half-duplex link.
The stop and wait ARQ may cause big performance issues as the sender always waits for
acknowledgement even if it has the next packet ready to send. Consider a situation where you
have a high bandwidth connection and propagation delay is also high (you are connected to some
server in some other country through a high-speed connection). To solve this problem, we can
send more than one packet at a time with a larger sequence number. So stop and wait ARQ may
work fine where propagation delay is very less for example LAN connections but performs badly
for distant connections like satellite connections.

Q31 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following languages given below:

 
 
Then, which of the following is/are true regarding L1 ∩ L2.
Correct answer: A, B, C, D
Solution

(a, b, c, d)
L 1 = {ab, a2b, a2b2, ab2 ...}
Simply it’s any number of a followed by any number of b and smallest string possible is ab. The
end of every string should be b
L 2 = {aa, aba, aabba, ...}
Here smallest possible string is aa. Here every string end with a.
Clearly, L1 ∩ L2 = { }
Ans so it is regular, CFL, CSL and recursive enumerable.

Q39 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is correct?

Correct answer: C, D
Solution

(c, d)
In option (c), they told that the language is intersection of two languages. The property of first
language is a’s is followed by b’s, and the property of second language is number of a’s and
number of b’s are equal. Their intersection means the resultant language should have both the
properties, which is nothing but set A.

Q40 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following assertions about viable prefixes is/are true?

Correct answer: A, B, D
Solution

(a, b, d)
A feasible prefix is always a prefix to the handle, as its name also implies. It cannot be suffixed to
a handle since the parser will take reduction action as soon as a handle reaches the top of the
stack, reducing it to the LHS side of the matched production.

Q43 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Suppose that the inclusive cache hierarchy has two levels, L1 and L2, with L2 being the higher level. Take into account the following claims. Then, which of the following is/are true?

Correct answer: B, C, D
Solution

(b, c, d)
S1 : Write through Cache refers to simultaneous access for both L1 and L2, and in this example, if
a read miss occurred in L1 cache, we attempted to retrieve it from L2 cache; however, if it was
dirty, we were not required to write back to L2 because Write through cache was in effect.
S2 : Writing across the cache is not permitted under the write allocate policy.

Q50 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A depth-first forest classifies the edges of a graph into tree, back, forward, and cross edges. A breadth-first tree can also be used to classify the edges reachable from the source of the search into the same four categories. In a breadth-first search of an undirected graph, which of the
following properties hold?

Correct answer: A, B
Solution

(a, b)
If we discovered a back edge, it meant that there were two vertices, one of which was a descendant
of the other, but there was already a path connecting the ancestor and the descendant that didn’t
need climbing the tree. There can be no other paths to that kid because doing so would make it
more than a single edge away, making this a contradiction. The only children in the bfs tree are
those that are a single edge away. The same approach is used to verify that there are no forward
edges. If a vertex had a forward edge, it would indicate that we have already processed one of its
children. However, since all children are only one edge, this is not possible.
If an edge leads to a vertex that has not yet been taken into account, it is added to the list of edges
to be processed. It follows that the distance between that vertex and the root must be at least the
current vertex’s distance plus 11. Since we may just follow the path that entails going to the
current vertex and following its path to the root, it is also at most that.
Because it would have been utilized as a tree edge when we were processing that earlier element,
we are aware that a cross edge cannot go deeper than one less. Additionally, it can’t be heading
to a vertex that is more than one depth down because we wouldn’t have processed a vertex that
was that far from the root before. Since the cross edge’s vertices cannot be more than one apart in
depth, the conclusion is reached by potentially switching the functions of u and v, which is possible
because the edges are unordered.

Q55 NAT Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Each process = 0, 1, 2, 3, ....., 9 is coded as follows:

 
 
The code for P10 is identical except that it uses V(mutex) instead of P(mutex), and P(mutex) instead of V(mutex). What is the largest number of processes that can be inside the critical section at any moment (the mutex being initialized to 1)?
NAT
Correct answer: 3
Solution

3
Following the execution of P(mutex), which reduces the mutex value to 0, any one of the nine
processes can enter the crucial area. P10 can now enter the crucial region by raising the value to 1.
By once more lowering the mutex value to 0, any one of the nine processes can now enter the
critical region. No process that is still running can reach its crucial stages.

Q57 MSQ Not checked

Not attempted

Consider the following grammar:

Then, which of the following statement(s) is/are true?

Correct answer: A, C, D
Solution

(a, c, d)
LR(1) grammar does not have any conflict while taking union of productions
M → c
N → c
It generates reduce/reduce conflict (RR Conflict).
Hence, the grammar is not LALR(1) grammar.
The grammar is LR(1) but not LALR(1). So not even SLR(1).

Suppose that the transaction T1 and T2 access the same database items.

 
 
Which one of the following statements is correct about these two transactions?
Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
First T1 updates the value of X and then T2 overwrites the value of X written by T1.
So the update made by T1 is lost.
Hence, a lost update problem.

Q60 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A priority queue Q is used to implement a stack S that stores characters. PUSH(C) is implemented as INSERT(Q, C, K) where K is an appropriate integer key chosen by the implementation. POP is implemented as DELETEMIN(Q). For a sequence of operations, the keys chosen are in

 
 
[Note: Duplicates not allowed.]
Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
When utilizing a priority queue, the first element added must have the highest priority in order
for the key to be in decreasing order. This is because the first element added will be eliminated
last when using the deletemin( ) action of the queue.
